00029 
00030 #ifndef _ADDRSPACE_H_
00031 #define _ADDRSPACE_H_
00032 
00033 /*
00034  * Address space structure and operations.
00035  */
00036 
00037 
00038 #include <vm.h>
00039 #include "opt-dumbvm.h"
00040 
00041 struct vnode;
00042 
00043 
00044 /* 
00045  * Address space - data structure associated with the virtual memory
00046  * space of a process.
00047  *
00048  * You write this.
00049  */
00050 
00051 struct addrspace {
00052 #if OPT_DUMBVM
00053         vaddr_t as_vbase1;
00054         paddr_t as_pbase1;
00055         size_t as_npages1;
00056         vaddr_t as_vbase2;
00057         paddr_t as_pbase2;
00058         size_t as_npages2;
00059         paddr_t as_stackpbase;
00060 #else
00061         /* Put stuff here for your VM system */
00062 #endif
00063 };
00064 
00065 /*
00066  * Functions in addrspace.c:
00067  *
00068  *    as_create - create a new empty address space. You need to make 
00069  *                sure this gets called in all the right places. You
00070  *                may find you want to change the argument list. May
00071  *                return NULL on out-of-memory error.
00072  *
00073  *    as_copy   - create a new address space that is an exact copy of
00074  *                an old one. Probably calls as_create to get a new
00075  *                empty address space and fill it in, but that's up to
00076  *                you.
00077  *
00078  *    as_activate - make the specified address space the one currently
00079  *                "seen" by the processor. Argument might be NULL, 
00080  *                meaning "no particular address space".
00081  *
00082  *    as_destroy - dispose of an address space. You may need to change
00083  *                the way this works if implementing user-level threads.
00084  *
00085  *    as_define_region - set up a region of memory within the address
00086  *                space.
00087  *
00088  *    as_prepare_load - this is called before actually loading from an
00089  *                executable into the address space.
00090  *
00091  *    as_complete_load - this is called when loading from an executable
00092  *                is complete.
00093  *
00094  *    as_define_stack - set up the stack region in the address space.
00095  *                (Normally called *after* as_complete_load().) Hands
00096  *                back the initial stack pointer for the new process.
00097  */
00098 
00099 struct addrspace *as_create(void);
00100 int               as_copy(struct addrspace *src, struct addrspace **ret);
00101 void              as_activate(struct addrspace *);
00102 void              as_destroy(struct addrspace *);
00103 
00104 int               as_define_region(struct addrspace *as, 
00105                                    vaddr_t vaddr, size_t sz,
00106                                    int readable, 
00107                                    int writeable,
00108                                    int executable);
00109 int               as_prepare_load(struct addrspace *as);
00110 int               as_complete_load(struct addrspace *as);
00111 int               as_define_stack(struct addrspace *as, vaddr_t *initstackptr);
00112 
00113 
00114 /*
00115  * Functions in loadelf.c
00116  *    load_elf - load an ELF user program executable into the current
00117  *               address space. Returns the entry point (initial PC)
00118  *               in the space pointed to by ENTRYPOINT.
00119  */
00120 
00121 int load_elf(struct vnode *v, vaddr_t *entrypoint);
00122 
00123 
00124 #endif /* _ADDRSPACE_H_ */
